figures.caption: Figure 2 Comparison of UCRL, COMPLETE, O-O UCRL-VTR (Optimal), and O-O UCRL-VTR (Pessimistic) under different environment shifts Δ, coverage 𝜏, 𝐾, and 𝑀off .
Figure 4 Supplementary results to Figure 2, including DP-LSVI and UCBVI. The setting in Figure 4a matches that of Figure 2a, and the setting in Figure 4b matches that of Figure 2b.

Figure 1 Graphical illustration of the sufficient conditions over the informativeness of offline data for 𝐾 = 103, 𝑀off = 1.5× 1011, 𝜖 = 10−3, 𝐻 = 3, 𝑑 = 5, and 𝐵 = 10. The admissible region is computed directly from Eq. (13). When 𝜏 ≲ 10−7, no value of Δ yields informative offline data. Once 𝜏 exceeds this threshold, larger 𝜏 admits a larger environment shift Δ, with an approximately linear dependence. The deviation from linearity near 𝜏 ≈ 10−7 is mainly caused by the logarithmic confidence term in Eq. (13).
Figure 3 Cumulative regret versus 𝜏 for different Δ(𝜏), with 𝐾 = 4× 103 and 𝑀off = 105.
